steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Text Structure and Purpose question asking for the function of the underlined sentence within the passage as a whole. The underlined sentence says “It may be tempting to assume the difference is due to different levels of access to parks.” The word “however” that immediately follows signals that the text will challenge this assumption. The correct answer must capture this setup-then-refute structure.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It introduces a counterexample to the scenario described earlier in the text.: The underlined sentence does not present a counterexample to earlier findings; it introduces a possible explanation that will itself be countered. ✗
  • Choice B — It provides context to help understand the scope of the researchers' survey.: The underlined sentence says nothing about the scope or methodology of the survey; it proposes an explanation for the usage difference. ✗
  • Choice C — It marks a shift from a discussion of the researchers' conclusion to a discussion of their methods.: The passage never discusses research methods in detail, and the underlined sentence is not about methods at all. ✗
  • Choice D — It presents a potential explanation for the team's findings that the text goes on to refute.: The underlined sentence offers a tempting but incomplete explanation (access to parks), and the clause beginning with “however” directly refutes it by showing proximity alone does not account for the difference. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
